c.d: The range of operating systems and devices on which the app is available.
c.s: Official app websites, App Store listings

c.d: The variety and depth of functionalities offered by the app, such as multimedia support, collaboration tools, and integration capabilities.
The design, ease of use, and overall user experience provided by the app.
The cost structure of the app, including free tiers, subscription plans, and one-time purchases.
The measures taken by the app to ensure user data security and privacy, such as encryption and compliance with regulations.
The app's ability to sync notes across devices and provide backup options to prevent data loss.
The extent to which the app allows customization of its interface and functionality, including support for plugins and themes.
The app's capability to function without an internet connection, allowing users to access and edit notes offline.
The tools and functionalities that enable multiple users to work together on notes or projects within the app.
The app's ability to connect and work seamlessly with other applications and services, such as cloud storage, calendars, and task managers.
